title: Populate ToolCall.description for Brain and Engine chat paths
body:
Issue #321 wired tool descriptions into the Kody Direct thinking panel via a single `data-tools-index` event at the start of the stream and an optional `description?` slot on `ToolCall` / `Message.toolCalls` (see `src/dashboard/lib/components/kody-chat-types.ts:28-35` and `:93-100`, `src/dashboard/lib/components/ToolCallCard.tsx:21-28` + `:111-115`, and `app/api/kody/chat/kody/route.ts` for the server side). Kody Direct now shows the description the model used to pick the tool under the tool name in the collapsed thinking-panel card.

Brain and Engine chats still don't populate `description`. Two payload gaps:

1. **Brain route** (`app/api/kody/chat/brain/route.ts`) — Brain has its own tool catalog on the Brain server, and the current stream surfaces tool_use blocks with name + input only. Brain would need to (a) ship a one-shot name→description map for the active tool set on its WebSocket/stream open, and (b) wire it into the dashboard's `chat.tool_use` event handler in `KodyChat.tsx` (around line ~2584 where `{ name, arguments, status: 'success' }` is pushed) so the new `description` field is set the same way Kody Direct does it. The dashboard-side change is mechanical: add a `chat.tools_index` event handler that fills `toolDescriptionByName`, then look up by name in the `chat.tool_use` branch.

2. **Engine event stream** (`app/api/kody/events/stream/route.ts:354`) — Engine runs in `kody2/src/chat/loop.ts` and writes its tool-call events to `.kody/events/{sessionId}.jsonl`. The dashboard consumes them via `chat.tool_use` events on the engine stream. The engine would need to (a) emit a `chat.tools_index` event at stream-open with the full name→description map, and (b) populate the `description` field on each `chat.tool_use` event from the same map. The dashboard-side change mirrors Brain: handle the new event, then attach `description` in the `chat.tool_use` branch (around `KodyChat.tsx:2584-2590`).

Acceptance criteria (same as #321, broadened):
- A Brain turn that calls a tool shows the tool's description (clipped to 140 chars) under the tool name in the thinking panel, without expanding the card.
- An Engine turn (default kody-live) shows the tool's description in the same slot.
- The `description?` field on the dashboard `ToolCall`/`Message.toolCalls` type stays optional throughout — only the Kody Direct path populates it today.
- No regression: arguments, result, status, duration, expand/collapse unchanged.
- One event per stream (full map), not one per call.

Out of scope for this follow-up: changing Brain's tool catalog itself or the engine's tool schema — both already carry descriptions; we just need to plumb them to the dashboard.
rationale: Issue #321 explicitly leaves Brain and Engine as deferred. The `description?` slot is already in the dashboard types and the card renders it gracefully when missing, so the only work is the upstream payload change in each backend plus the matching client consumer update. Picking this up later means Brain/Engine users keep seeing tool name + args + result with no human-readable explanation of why the model chose the tool.
